meta_notes: News shelf empty by design: the only major dermatology story (bemotrizinol FDA approval) broke in June with an Aug 9 effective date - the playbook's own >7d recap-trap, excluded. Fresher candidates (TikTok sunscreen-misinformation study, 'tanmaxxing' warnings) had no verifiable <=7d dateline (client-rendered shells), so none included rather than guessing. Kids-anti-aging news is already saturated in the feed - folded into the top stitch, not news. Feeds fresh (extract 2026-07-20).

stitch_shortlist.author: @itvnews
stitch_shortlist.claim: Kids as young as 4 using adult anti-aging skincare

pitch.the_claim: Children 4+ are using adult anti-aging actives; dermatologists want warning labels and tougher scrutiny.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.1M news clip (ITV) with derms already calling for regulation - a claim + a cause in one.
pitch.your_fit: She's a mom-derm; corpus has an anti-aging series + skincare gifts but NO 'kids shouldn't use actives' video - open lane, needs her take on the pediatric-skin specifics.
angles: empathy-first: parents just want to help their kids
'you want me to put acid on a 4-year-old?'
what young skin actually needs: sunscreen, gentle moisturizer, done

pitch.the_claim: A DIY zinc-oxide mask, presented as a skincare tip.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.9M, and it rides the exact zinc-oxide moment she is already winning this week.
pitch.your_fit: Her zinc-oxide diaper-cream barrier hack is a 5% breakout THIS week (171K) - a perfect duet; she owns the on-topic angle.
angles: yes-and: zinc oxide IS great - here's the RIGHT way to use it
switch-don't-quit: skip the DIY mask, here's a tested one
barrier vs sunscreen: don't confuse the two

stitch_shortlist.author: @sierrajarratt
stitch_shortlist.claim: 'I bathe in tret, slather it everywhere'

pitch.the_claim: Slathering tretinoin all over the body, daily, 'everywhere'.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.3M glorifying tret overuse - irritation, barrier damage, pregnancy-risk red flags.
pitch.your_fit: She teaches retinoids as pea-sized, buffered, and NOT whole-body - a clean correction lane (needs her exact dosing take on camera).
angles: 'you want me to WHAT?': tret is not body lotion
empathy-first: I get the glow chase - here's the safe dose
the buffering / sandwich method

pitch.your_fit: Sun protection is her most-covered topic; she can celebrate + add the UPF-fabric science.

the swimsuit truth: fabric UPF beats reapplying on your back
who else quietly does this

stitch_shortlist.claim: Benzoyl peroxide wash for underarm odor

pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.0M, useful and mostly right - a fellow MD, so the play is yes-and, not correction.
pitch.your_fit: She covers body acne and BP washes; amplify + add the nuance he skipped.
angles: yes-and: he's right - here's WHY it works
add: antiperspirant vs deodorant, and bleached shirts warning
who should NOT (sensitive / broken skin)

stitch_shortlist.author: @drjessicaburgy
stitch_shortlist.claim: Nail melanoma = dark band, not a blotch

pitch.the_claim: Nail melanoma usually shows as a dark longitudinal band, not a blotch; a bruise (subungual hematoma) follows trauma and grows out.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.07M, correct and important - amplify a fellow derm and fold it into today's skin-cancer wave.
pitch.your_fit: Her BCC/melanoma guides (493K) give her the authority; add the Hutchinson sign and when to biopsy.
angles: yes-and: she's right - here's the Hutchinson sign to watch
the nail stripe to worry about vs the harmless ones
when a dark nail line needs a dermatologist NOW
